### Post by poofyhairguy on 2005-05-07
[QUOTE=bored2k]Ok so point me in the right direction to some of his work. [/QUOTE]

Well, the best album he had with the band (I have them all) was Whatever and Ever and Amen. I've listened to that CD like 200 times. The band had three other albums.

Then he has two by himself- Rocking the Suburbs and Songs for Silverman.

Off of these I really like the Ascent of Stan (textboot hippy man) and Jesusland.

He also put out some redo's of some of his earlier tracks recently. Very nice, was a good snack before the meal of the new album came through.

Trent and Ben are my favorite of all time....

### Post by bored2k on 2005-05-15
[QUOTE=panickedthumb]Norah Jones and Alicia Keys? Old people music? *L*

But yeah, I love Norah. Alicia Keys is good, but not really my thing for some reason.

Where do you live again bored? How easy is it to keep up with the American music scene? I've been getting more and more into the British music scene lately, and that's not too easy to keep up with from America. You aren't flooded with it daily.[/QUOTE]
 **sorry for extending your question a lil bizzle*

I live in the Dominican Republic. It's very easy to keep up with the "gringo" music scene and everything else in general. Most of the people [about 80%] barely know english and listen just to their crappy -20 times more obnoxious than folk music- "bachata" music. But teens like me going from medium-high economic class and above get **very** influenced by american music. We listen to all the music you guys listen. Now.. ignorant fashion bubble head chumps just like whatever's mainstream, but some of us are really into good music. We don't care if its mainstream or not, we just dig good music. Here we have two types of american music listeners: " ignorant weak pathetic fools " that just follow the trend, and people that like what they hear [old school gansta rap like nwa; at-the-drive-in, tool, nin, etc. A lot of us have the 'this country sucks big time' culture so we tend to follow another country's "life" ;). There's no country music lovers here though [thank God]. Some even watch the superbowl like they're native from us or something, but that's just playing stupid (main sport is baseball). Our television is filled with about 90% of U.S.A. channels, maybe 7% dominican channels, and 3% foreign like french or german.

And btw, dominican republic and the caribbean is in America, so that makes us americans too ;). YOu would mean north-americans :-P.


"O'reilly books : Dominican Republic in a nutshell"= bananas + beaches . yes you could this donkey kong's lair..

British music ? we don't get that hear unless it gets mainstream attention in the US ;) lol

### Post by Havoc on 2005-05-23
**[COLOR=Black]BLACK SABBATH[/COLOR] - [COLOR=DarkRed]DIE YOUNG[/COLOR]**

In my opinion, one of the best songs "old" Heavy Metal has to offer...

Again, IMHO, music is growing stale....Ok, you hear catchy tunes, but there is no real variety...Only a couple of bands trying to do something different.Musically, only downtuned guitars are really used (Guitars with a heavy sound), and people don't pay attention to things like time changes (The change of tempo and general tune in a song).Lyrics are very shallow, dealing with topics like Women, Sex, Love, Alchohol and pseudo-rebel-ism.IMO, the best of times for Rock and (the then-emerging) Heavy Metal were the 60s - late 80s/early 90s.Since then, not many are holding the flag for originality and creationism.IMHO, that is.   :|

### Post by TravisNewman on 2005-05-26
Pearl Jam was amazing. Ironic that they came out in the grunge revolution brought into the foreground by Nirvana-- but these bands, Nirvana, Pearl Jam, Soundgarden, heck even Green Day can be thrown into it. But they all sounded so DIFFERENT. 

Today, a band starts a trend and instantly there are all these cookie cutter replicas, when just over 10 years ago, there was real, raw, and original music.

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-05-26
[QUOTE=panickedthumb]Pearl Jam was amazing. Ironic that they came out in the grunge revolution brought into the foreground by Nirvana-- but these bands, Nirvana, Pearl Jam, Soundgarden, heck even Green Day can be thrown into it. But they all sounded so DIFFERENT. 

Today, a band starts a trend and instantly there are all these cookie cutter replicas, when just over 10 years ago, there was real, raw, and original music.[/QUOTE]
 Ok Green Day is probably the rock band I respect/like/heard the most. They might not be the best, but I have listened to their albums like no other. I still remember when I first saw the video for Basket Case I was about 7. I was completely shocked because I had really never liked any music before that. I liked them so much I remember listening to the tapes and hand-copying the lyrics (I had no idea they were available..heck, I think I didnt even have a computer).

From the bands you mentioned, I just know/like the ones that got uber popular (Black Hole Sun, Jeremy.. my bro loved Nirvana so I know a few).

What's a good Pearl Jam album ?

haggard - All inizio e la morte

> 
All'inizio E La Morte

[To The Beginning And The Dead]

[Music: A. Nasseri / Lyrics: A. Nasseri]

Tremens factus sum ego [I shake because of the facts]
Et timeo [and I'm scared]
Dum discussio [while I talk]
Venerit at que ventura ira [it will come to the upcomig wrath]
Et timeo [and I'm scared]
Dum discussio [while I talk]
Venerit at que ventura ira [it will come to the upcoming wrath]

Herbei, ihr Leut' - das Fest beginnt [come run up you people, the celebration starts]
Nehmt Platz auf besten Rängen [take your seats on best places]
Seht, wie der flehend Mann dort kniet [see how the begging man kneels there]
Seht den Ketzer, ich sag, er soll brennen heut' Nacht [see the heretic, tonight he shall burn I say]
Soll ihn spür'n, den Tod, er kommt langsam und lacht [(he) should feel death, it comes slowly and laughs]
Und als die Töchter des Teufels er nennt [and as he tells about Devil's daughters]
Die Sense den Faden des Lebens zertrennt [the scythe severes the thread of life]

Und als sein letzter Schrei erstirbt [and when his final scream dies]
Die Flammen ihn verschlingen [flames consume him]
Und Kindesweinen bricht die Still.... [children's crying breaks the silence...]
Seht zur Asche ward er, der die Lügen erzählt [see to ashes he went who told those lies]
Im Zeichen des Kreuzes [under the sign of the cross]
Hat er seinen Tod selbst gewählt [he chose his death himself]
Doch unsere Seelen sind rein... [but our souls are pure...]

Doch unsere Seelen sind rein... [but our souls are pure...]

Schenkt aus den Wein! [pour out the wine]

Profundis Clamavi [endlessly we cry out]
And as the dark procession then has been fulfilled
For their god - a sacrifice - his blood was spilled
So Death took him to mother's chest
Where he may rest...

And the weather says, it's springtime
See the leafs of darkest green
And his screams hall in the nightly breeze
As the darkened sorrows feels

Dark are all the autumn nights
Where mind and stars unite
Knowing forbidden wisdom
And all the things he sees
Collected in a chamber
Secrets of astronomy

The rage in their eyes
Torches in their hands
And the power of the cross
Bringing fear to all the land
... and darkness will come to us all!

Profundis Clamavi! [endlessly we cry out]

And as the dark procession...

Schenkt aus den Wein! [pour out the wine]

### Post by sapo on 2005-06-02
Dream Theater/Images And Words (1992)/05 - Metropolis - Part I 'The Miracle And The Sleeper'.mp3

Best song ever! [img]http://ubuntuforums.org/images/smilies/eusa_dance.gif[/img]

> 
[Images: Dream Theater]
[Words: Petrucci]

The smile of dawn
Arrived early May
She carried a gift from her
home
The night shed a tear
To tell her of fear
And of sorrow and pain
She'll never outgrow

Death is the first dance, eternal

There's no more freedom
The both of you will be
confined to this mind

I was told there's miracle for
each day that I try
I was told there's a new love
that's born for each one that
has died
I was told there'd be no one to
call on when I feel alone and
afraid
I was told if you dream of the
next world
You'll find yourself swimming
in a lake of fire

As a child, I thought I could
live without pain without
sorrow
As a man I've found it's all
caught up with me
I'm asleep yet I'm so afraid

Somewhere like a scene from a
memory
There's a picture worth a
thousand words
Eluding stares from faces
before me
It hides away and will never be
heard of again

Deceit is the second without end

The city's cold blood teaches us
to survive
Just keep my heart in your eyes
and we'll stay alive

The third arrives...

Before the leaves have fallen
Before we lock the doors
There must be the third and
last dance
This one will last forever
Metropolis watches and
thoughtfully smiles
She's taken you to your home

It can only take place
When the struggle between
our children has ended
Now the Miracle and the
Sleeper know that the third
is love

Love is the Dance of Eternity

### Post by weekend warrior on 2005-06-11
Ravi Coltrane - [Mad 6](http://www.amazon.com/exec/obidos/ASIN/B00008WT4H/qid=1118504303/sr=2-2/ref=pd_bbs_b_2_2/104-9259875-3128741),  yes he is the son of you know who... but he's no Julian Lennon papa wannabe! Ravi's paid his dues and has his own voice. His third disc, fierce up front and funky is what Mad6 is about. I listen to this when I need a jazz caffeine kick.

[IMG]http://www.village-records.com/88/e/discogra/image/mad6_img.gif[/IMG] 

Have to admire Ravi for keeping his head on straight despite being the son of one of the most singular musical minds in history (and named after yet another!). Did someone say high expectations?!? He's not afraid to take on his father's legacy either, like on "26-2" and "Fifth House". I'd even dare say Ravi does father John one up on soprano sax. Angular, straight up, on the rocks modern jazz (no easy-listening Kenny G pseudo-jazz here!) from someone who has it in his blood. Mad 6 is a fascinating angular step in Ravi's evolution following in his father's "giant steps". 

This little mini-review is especially for bored2k :) for braving the jazz waters. A much better review [here](http://www.allaboutjazz.com/php/article.php?id=12054)  and [here](http://www.popmatters.com/music/reviews/c/coltraneravi-mad.shtml)

### Post by weekend warrior on 2005-06-20
> I'm embarassed that I can't tell which language specifically 
**Weekend Warriors Basic Crash Course for Telling Apart Japanese and Chinese**

It's relatively easy to distinguish Japanese and Chinese once you know what to look for. Look at the examples below and pay attention to the last characters like &#12511; &#12481; &#12523; or &#12424; &#12358; &#23376;. Do you see how simplified they are? These are Japanese hiragana syllabary. Each one stands for a consonant and vowel (e.g. Ko-Na-Mi) instead of an entire word. Chinese doesn't have these at all. Japanese uses them extensively because Japanese and Chinese are night and day grammatically and phonetically - as different as English to Swahili. 

So as you can see, the first examples here are Japanese, In the 5th one you can also see katakana, this is a script only for foreign names, it looks more angular. Then after these Japanese examples are Chinese ones. Notice how Chinese looks more complex and compact? It doesn't have all those hiragana/katakana characters like Japanese. 

[hiragana table](http://www.japan-guide.com/e/e2047.html ) , [katakana table](http://www.japan-guide.com/e/e2048.html) 

&#33733;&#37326;&#12424;&#12358;&#23376; .......  &#12424;&#12358;&#23376; = hiragana characters

&#23665;&#26681;&#12511;&#12481;&#12523;

&#33733;&#37326;&#12424;&#12358;&#23376;

&#27211;&#26412;&#19968;&#23376;

&#12473;&#12497;&#12540;&#12474;&#12289;2&#23395;&#12406;&#12426;&#12398;&#20778;&#21213;&#12395;&#29579;&#25163; .......  &#12473;&#12497;&#12540;&#12474; = katakana       

&#26085;&#26412;&#22269;&#20869;&#12391;&#30330;&#34892;&#12373;&#12428;&#12383;&#20837;&#25163;&#21487;&#33021;&#12394;&#26360;&#31821;&#12434;&#35519;&#12409;&#12425;&#12428;&#12427;&#24773;&#22577;&#12469;&#12452;&#12488;&#12290;
               
________________________________________________

&#38597;&#34382;&#20013;&#25991;&#26032;&#38395;

&#22307;&#36335;&#26131;&#26102;&#25253;

&#26032;&#32862;&#12289;&#23067;&#27138;&#12289;&#21103;&#21002;&#12289;&#29289;&#26989;&#31199;&#21806;&#36039;&#26009;&#24235;&#12289;&#32178;&#19978;&#25512;&#37559;&#24291;&#22580;&#31561;&#22577;&#36947;&#12290;


Do you see the difference now?


HTH

### Post by weekend warrior on 2005-06-22
You know it just occurred to me that my post above on Japanese and Chinese for panickedthumb probably looks like gobbledygook for some people. If you want to read it and it looks like gibberish then get Asian fonts support on your box! 

Anyway, right now I'm listening to one of the most incredible discs to ever come out of Brasil: "Tropicalia 2 - Caetano e Gil". Whenever I pull this out I'm reminded how varied and timesless it is. I've had this disc for 10+ years and just don't grow tired of it. How could you! Caetano Veloso and Gilberto Gil, two masters together! Tropicalia 2 is a precious gem you shouldn't pass over if you come across it. And btw the lyrics are translated into English for those of you that can't follow Portuguese - not that you have to, you'll be happy enough even if you don't understand uma palavra.


[IMG]http://images.amazon.com/images/P/B000005J2O.01._SCMZZZZZZZ_.jpg[/IMG]

### Post by TheRealEdwin on 2005-06-22
[QUOTE=panickedthumb]well, the songs that I can read on your scrobbler I like a lot.

You have any english translations for the ones that are oriental (I'm embarassed that I can't tell which language specifically) so that I might be able to find them?

I noticed Nobuo Uematsu in there. I love the Final Fantasy music.[/QUOTE]
 You have to have an account and change the setting in your options menu.

Audioscrobbler is a wonderful little plug in for players as it suggest what music you might like (always spot for me), tracks your music, shows you what your neighbors are listening to, stats, rankings, and it ties in with [www.last.fm](www.last.fm).

Last.fm is a web radio site that plays music similar to what you like so you can try the music out before you obtain it yourself. Many good songs have been discovered by this wonderful service. We should start a ubuntuforums group too.

edit: To hell with it, I made it. [http://www.audioscrobbler.com/group/Ubuntu%20Forums](http://www.audioscrobbler.com/group/Ubuntu%20Forums)

### Post by alred on 2005-07-02
Carl Orff's Carmina Burana

quote :: 
"... Carmina Burana or "Songs of Benediktbeuern" is a collection of 13th-century stories, poems and songs which was discovered in 1803 at a monestary in Beuern, Bavaria, and has become one of the best-known sources for medieval European literature ... 
... appeal of Orff's music lies in the way it uncovers and reasserts something of music's "reason," its primordial and instinctual qualities. There is an innocent, unselfconscious quality to Carmina. We respond to its eternal, elemental truths in the spirit of medieval theologian/poet Peter Abelard, who urged making "no more new songs of the mysteries of philosophy, but of love's secrets only."

they are sequenced classical songs in midi format ...

goto this web site and have a look :: [Carmina Burana MIDI Files](http://www.botproductions.com/music/carmina.html)

### Post by SuperMike on 2005-10-02
I've been out of the loop from net radio for some time now. Just too much work to do and a family to raise. I needed some background noise while moonlighting on a project. I also didn't understand Linux's take on the whole net radio thing, so I decided to poke around. I found this smgradio.com from Virgin Records. They have an Ogg Vorbis streaming audio radio station thing and they have cool British DJs come on. I like the music coming from over there right now, anyway. I didn't realize this Ogg Vorbis thing was just as good as any other streaming audio format. When I clicked on the link, a program I didn't even know I had in Ubuntu, Rhythmbox, opened up and started streaming it pretty effectively. I was disappointed that occasionally it hangs, but it's not very often.

If you have some other sites you'd like to recommend to me, let me know. I'd like to hear something mixing Brazilian Bossa Nova with something new and modern, but in a really cool way. Or perhaps some new Radiohead-sounding underground kind of thing, really well done.

---

### Post by eriqk on 2006-02-14
[QUOTE=Krigl]Death In June - Rose Clouds of Holocaust

Any DI6 fans here?[/QUOTE]

### Post by luna6 on 2006-04-16
Some new stuff that I have been listening to feverishly....

1.) Voxtrtot - Mothers, Sisters, Daughters & Wives (Combines some buzzcocks meets Belle & Sebastian type of melodies....Austin, TX band)

2) Elf Power - Back To The Web (Out on April 25 -- sounds like some of Elliott Smiths records and a little bit of REM from Life's Rich Pagent)

3) The Watson Twins - Southern Manners (indie rock meets sultry country sounds)

4) I'm From Barcelona - We're From Barcelona (some of the catchiest songs ...and the band is from Sweden...go figure)

5) Nathan Fake - Drowning In A Sea Of Love (best electronic album of the year...some fourt tet'ish electronics meets M83 type of sounds...very accessible and addictive)

### Post by Abild on 2006-05-12
Right now its Immortal - Sons Of Northern Darkness - In My Kingdom Cold

True Norwegian Black Metal \m/

One of the best bands to have touched the face of this earth. Sadly, they disbanded in 2003. After that Abbath started a new band called Zyklon. Their first album was amazing black metal mixed with a bit death. It also featured one of my fav vocalists Daemon. In their second album Daemon left and Secthdamon took over the vocals and they started playing standard death metal :(.

### Post by B0rsuk on 2006-06-11
Deep Purple from last.fm (Rat Bat Blue)

If you don't know what [http://last.fm](http://last.fm) is, it's a very nice internet radio station. It's interactive so you can skip music, ban music, or declare love for individual pieces meaning you will hear it more often.

It uses GPL licence for their standalone player and sourcecode is available. It uses Creative Commons licenses for other stuff.
It's highly customizable and you get access to lots of statistics.
In my opinion, it tends to play much better music than pandora. I used both for a long time. 

To my suprise, they not only support linux, but offer a wide choice of player plugins, standalone player... and amarok even comes with built-in last.fm support by default.
Oh, and it's free. There are some features you'd need to pay for, but you can have lots of fun without paying a penny.

### Post by justin whitaker on 2007-05-14
> **graabein said:**
> What Art Blakey albums do you recommend? I think I have one somewhere. Or did I just borrow it? Tips are welcome.

I'm listening to John Coltrane and Charles Mingus these days. And also rock like the Stones (way better than the Beatles!!) and the Saints.

[IMG]http://image.allmusic.com/00/amg/cov250/drd100/d136/d136869hi20.jpg[/IMG]

Giant Steps is one of my favorites.

For Blakey, try Art Blakey & The Jazz Messengers: A Night In Tunisa to start.

[http://www.amazon.com/Night-Tunisia-Blakey-Jazz-Messengers/dp/B00079ZA0Y/ref=pd_bbs_sr_2/002-6822752-3192823?ie=UTF8&s=music&qid=1179178276&sr=8-2](http://www.amazon.com/Night-Tunisia-Blakey-Jazz-Messengers/dp/B00079ZA0Y/ref=pd_bbs_sr_2/002-6822752-3192823?ie=UTF8&s=music&qid=1179178276&sr=8-2)

Blakey, Wayne Shorter, Lee Morgan,Bobby Timmons...classic set. 

Another good one is the Cafe Bohemia sessions, this time with Horace Silver taking over piano duties with the Messengers.

### Post by AndyCooll on 2007-07-18

### Post by AlexenderReez on 2007-07-19
i'm listening konayuki, japan's song....hm..here the translation -->


> Powdered Snow

The season in which the powdered snow dances always passes by
Even if I’m lost in a crowd I can see the same sky
Even though I'm chilled as if I'm being blown by the wind

I don't know anything(1) about you, do I?
And yet, I found you amongst a billion(2) people
There's no (scientific) basis for this, but I believe this with all seriousness

We can't live at the same time without trivial fights
If I can't be honest, then rapture and sorrow are meaningless

Powdered snow, if you paled me white to the heart
Can you share our loneliness?

I pressed my ear against your heart
Going gently, deeply towards where the sound (is coming from)
(That's where) I want to disembark,
There, we'll meet once again

I want us to reach rapport but I was the (only) one who touched its surface
The only thing that was holding us together was my hand squeezing yours that was numb with cold
Powdered snow, in front (of us), eternity, too fragilely, becomes a stain upon rough asphalt

Powdered snow, this heart that has transcended time is faltering
And yet, I want to continue to protect you

Powdered snow, if you paled me white to the heart
You would wrap around our loneliness and send it back into the sky

### Post by afljafa on 2007-09-10
The night is black, without a moon.
The air is thick and still.
The vigilantes gather on the lonely torchlit hill.

Features distorted in the flickering light, faces are twisted and grotesque.
Silent and stern in the sweltering night, the mob moves like demons possesed.
Quiet in conscience, calm in their right, confident their ways are best.

The righteous rise with burning eyes of hatred and ill-will.
Madmen fed on fear and lies to beat and burn and kill.

They say there are strangers who threaten us: our immigrants and infidels.
They say there is strangeness to danger us in our theatres and bookstore shelves.
Those who know what's best for us must rise and save us from ourselves!

Quick to judge, quick to anger, slow to understand.
Ignorance and prejudice and fear walk hand in hand...

### Post by got awesome? on 2008-01-03
Currently listening to:

**Little Wing - Lightheaded LP** - [discogs](http://www.discogs.com/release/43165)

A small release on Hardkiss Records, produced by Robbie Hardkiss (with a lil help from Gavin and Scott). It's a lovely example of electronic music in the mid 90's, it doesn't have the same kind of production you hear these days, but it's far groovier than that cheap electro/house stuff that's flooding the airwaves!!

**Young American Primitive - Young American Primitive** - [discogs](http://www.discogs.com/release/21439)

Another old release, with a track featured on Sasha & Digweed's first "Northern Exposure" release. A beautiful album, with the kind of rhythmic trance that is all but lost in todays electronic music. Oh, how I wish I could hear this stuff when I go out!

:)

> **LaRoza said:**

### Post by Tenken on 2008-01-10
It's a cover of two songs (House Rent Blues and One Bourbon, One Scotch, One Beer) by John Lee Hooker
```

Wanna tell you a story,
about the house-rent blues
I come home one Friday,
had to tell the landlady I'd-a lost my job
She said that don't confront me,
long as I get my money next Friday
Now next Friday come I didn't get the rent,
and out the door I went

So I goes to the landlady,
I said, "You let me slide?"
I'll have the rent for you tomorrow.
the next day I don't know
So said let me slide it on you know people,
I notice when I come home in the evening
She ain't got nothing nice to say to me,
but for five year she was so nice
Loh' she was lovy-dovy,
I come home one particular evening
The landlady said, "You got the rent money yet?",
I said, "No, can't find no job"
Therefore I ain't got no money to pay the rent
She said "I don't believe you're tryin' to find no job"
Said "I seen you today you was standin' on a corner,
leaning up against a post"
I said "But I'm tired, I've been walkin' all day"
She said "That don't confront me,
long as I get my money next Friday"
Now next Friday come I didn't have the rent,
and out the door I went

So I go down the streets,
down to my good friend's house
I said "Look man I'm outdoors you know,
can I stay with you maybe a couple days?"
He said "Uh, Let me go and ask my wife"
He come out of the house,
I could see in his face
I know that was no
He said "I don't know man, ah she kinda funny, you know"
I said "I know, everybody funny, now you funny too"
So I go back home
I tell the landlady I got a job, I'm gonna pay the rent
She said "Yeah?" I said "Oh yeah"
And then she was so nice,
loh' she was lovy-dovy
So I go in my room, pack up my things and I go,
I slip on out the back door and down the streets I go
She a-hollerin' about the front rent, she'll be lucky to get any back rent,
she ain't gonna get none of it
So I stop in the local bar you know people,
I go to the bar, I ring my coat, I call the bartender
Said "Look man, come down here", he got down there
So what you want?

One bourbon, one scotch, one beer
Well I ain't seen my baby since I don't know when,
I've been drinking bourbon, whiskey, scotch and gin
Gonna get high man I'm gonna get loose,
need me a triple shot of that juice
Gonna get drunk don't you have no fear
I want one bourbon, one scotch and one beer
One bourbon, one scotch, one beer

But I'm sitting now at the bar,
I'm getting drunk, I'm feelin' mellow
I'm drinkin' bourbon, I'm drinkin' scotch, I'm drinkin' beer
Looked down the bar, here come the bartender
I said "Look man, come down here"
So what you want?

One bourbon, one scotch, one beer
No I ain't seen my baby since the night before last,
gotta get a drink man I'm gonna get gassed
Gonna get high man I ain't had enough,
need me a triple shot of that stuff
Gonna get drunk won't you listen right here,
I want one bourbon, one shot and one beer
One bourbon, one scotch, one beer

Now by this time I'm plenty high,
you know when your mouth a-getting dry you're plenty high
Looked down the bar I say to my bartender
I said "Look man, come down here", he got down there
So what you want this time?
I said "Look man, a-what time is it?"
He said "The clock on the wall say three o'clock
Last call for alcohol, so what you need?"

One bourbon, one scotch, one beer
No I ain't seen my baby since a nigh' and a week,
gotta get drunk man till I can't even speak
Gonna get high man listen to me,
one drink ain't enough Jack you better make it three
I wanna get drunk I'm gonna make it real clear,
I want one bourbon, one scotch and one beer
One bourbon, one scotch, one beer
```
*Wanted Man (Live)* - Johnny Cash

### Post by Visti on 2008-01-10
I just recently fell in love with my The Smiths 2-CD Best Of again, so..

```
The Smiths - Heaven Knows I'm Miserable Now

I was happy in the haze of a drunken hour
But heaven knows I'm miserable now

I was looking for a job, and then i found a job
And heaven knows I'm miserable now

In my life
Why do i give valuable time
To people who don't care if i live or die ?

Two lovers entwined pass me by
And heaven knows I'm miserable now

I was looking for a job, and then i found a job
And heaven knows I'm miserable now

In my life
Oh, why do i give valuable time
To people who don't care if i live or die ?

What she asked of me at the end of the day
Caligula would have blushed

"oh, you've been in the house too long" she said
And i (naturally) fled

In my life
Why do i smile
At people who I'd much rather kick in the eye ?

I was happy in the haze of a drunken hour
But heaven knows I'm miserable now

"oh, you've been in the house too long" she said
And i (naturally) fled

In my life
Oh, why do i give valuable time
```


Now that's good stuff!

> **1.  	I Resemble That Remar**k

Snooty Rich Lady: "Murphy, who are those slovenly creatures at the door?"

Murphy the butler: The plumbers, Madam.

Snooty Rich Lady: Well, send them to the servant's entrance. We can't allow rifraff in the front door.

Curly: I Resemble That Remark !

Bored-- Ben Folds is one of the best musicians ever. ### Post by SirThom on 2008-04-27
> **aimran said:**
> What's lampi koala?

In Maylay, Lampi means like 'stupid' or 'slow'.

So instead of Koala Lumpur, it's Lampi Koala.

Koala being a marsupial often mistook for a bear.

At least that's what I've been told by a Malaysian friend that Lampi means.  I've used that joke with another Malaysian and she seemed to get it.

```

How many times can you put me down
'Till in your heart you realize
If you chose to criticize you choose your enemies

Everything that I say and do
In your eyes is always wrong
Tell me where do I belong in a sick society

You're no different to me, yeah
You're no different, no different to me

Look at yourself instead o' looking at me
With accusation in your eyes
Do you want me crucified for my profanity

Concealing your crimes behind a grandeur of lies

If you think you're without sin be the first to cast the stone

You're no different to me, yeah
You're no different, no different to me
You're no different to me, yeah
You're no different, no different to me

Can't you see, can't you see

### Post by money2themax on 2008-05-05
Amarok: I Don't Believe In Love by Queensrÿche on Operation: Mindcrime

```

I awoke on impact
Under surveillance from the camera eye
Searching high and low
The criminal mind found at the scene of the crime
Handcuffed and blind, I don't do it
She said she loved me
I guess I never knew
But do we ever, ever really know?
She said shed meet me on the other side
But I knew right then, Id never find her

I don't believe in love
I never have, I never will
I don't believe in love
Its never worth the pain that you feel

No more nightmares, Ive seen them all
From the day I was born, they've haunted my every move
Every open hands there to push and shove
No time for love it doesn't matter
She made a difference
I guess she had a way
Of making every night seem bright as day
Now I walk in shadows, never see the light
She must have lied cause she never said goodbye

I don't believe in love
I never have, I never will
I don't believe in love
Ill just pretend she never was real
I don't believe in love I need to forget her face, I see it still
I don't believe in love
Its never worth the pain that you feel

[COLOR="Red"]No chance for contact
Theres no Raison d'être
My only hope is one day I'll forget
The pain of knowing what can never be
With or without love its all the same to me

I don't believe in love[/COLOR]
I never have, I never will
I don't believe in love
Ill just pretend she never was real
I don't believe in love
I need to forget her face, I see it still
I don't believe in love
Its never worth the pain that you feel

```

Last night the word came down, ten dead in Chinatown.

### Post by brewstah on 2008-05-19
78th Fraser Highlanders - Live in Ireland

This is a great recording.  This is probably the best pipe & drum concert recording I've ever heard.

I suppose one should expect that from top bands like the 78th Frasers, Simon Fraser University, Schotts and Dykehead, etc.  What sets this apart from most of the rest is it doesn't start with synthesizers or some other "pop" intro....it's pure bagpipe goodness through and through. :)

> **Dr. Octagon said:**
> Polygon Window - If it Really is Me


[IMG]http://i6.photobucket.com/albums/y229/themilkcart/pw.jpg[/IMG]

Nice, one of my personal favorites from Aphex Twin.

Quite a coincidence, because I am listening to the artist "[The Tuss]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_tuss")" at this moment. The Tuss is part of the Replex label and is thought to be Richard James him self under pseudonym. It sounds identical to the Analord albums and a Yahmaha GX1 synthesizer is used, which is a very rare instrument that Richard owns.

Spoken:
You have come here from all over the world
Because society has no further use for you
This place [incomprehensible] your home and until your death
I'm very good to this place, in fact I call it [incomprehensibe]
But if you break any of my rules

Sang:
Patience is how I'm living today
I lie before you stressing me out again
My life is twisted, twisted like your own brains
And I can't accept that, And I can't accept that again

Yeah face it, no try it
Yeah immune to what you are

A superstition jinxing me like the black cat
Inside you it's only what I crave
A system breakdown, can you remember your name?
Did you forget you? Did you forget your identity?

Yeah face it, no try it
Yeah immune to what you are

Yeah face it, no try it
Yeah immune to what you are

Yeah try it
No yeah yeah

What's the matter with your own ways?
Why are you freakin' me out again? No
What's the matter with your own mind?
You can't be yourself anymore
When you're suckin' out my life from under me

Yeah face it, no try it
Yeah immune to what you are

Yeah face it, no try it
Yeah immune to what you are

Face it, yeah face it
No face it, yeah try

### Post by Th3Professor on 2008-06-13
> **Helios1276 said:**
> My neighbour+ his new speakers+ his rubbish taste in music*


*Trance

LOL! Yeah, that's actually why I had death metal playing earlier. Neighbors like to turn up some really horrible rap... there's some good rap out there but the stuff they were playing was absolute trash. Thankfully they have a weak, low quality, sound system/entertainment center. Mine blew them out of the water ;) ...and to give them a bonus I cranked the subwoofer.

I can stomach some death metal. I can stomach some rap.
The best bass, between just those two, is in death metal, hands down.

Participating in volume-wars, or what have you, is not generally something I do... though these neighbors have been incessantly play crappy music too loud for so long that I finally decided to let them experience a taste of their own medicine, let 'em have a taste of it.

One could only guess how they responded.

(And they couldn't turn theirs up any louder, their system was already maxed out.)

(There were no other neighbors around at the time. If there were, out of respect for them, I wouldn't have played the li'l volume game. I'd have just called the police.)

:guitar:

(guitar solo) 

while youre breakin down my back 
and ive been wrackin out my brains 
it don't matter how we make it 
cause it always ends the same 
you can push it for more mileage 
but your flaps are wearing thing 
and i could sleep on it til mornin 
but this nightmare never ends 
dont forget to call my lawyer with ridiculous demands 
and you can take the pity so far 
but its more than i can stand 
cause this couch trip's gettin older  
tell me how long has it been 
cause 5 years is forever and you havent grown up yet... 

(chorus) 

you could be 
you should be 
you could be mine 
(you could be mine) 
(repeat / rotating with backup vocals) 
YOU COULD BE MIIIIIIIIIIIIIIINNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNEEE YEAH!!!!! 

```

Ding-a-ling-ling-ling-ling-ling-ling-ling!

### Post by money2themax on 2008-10-27
Learning To Fly by Pink Floyd on A Momentary Lapse Of Reason

```

Into the distance, a ribbon of black
Stretched to the point of no turning back
A flight of fancy on a windswept field
Standing alone my senses reeled
A fatal attraction holding me fast, how
Can I escape this irresistible grasp? 

Cant keep my eyes from the circling skies
Tongue-tied and twisted just an earth-bound misfit, i

Ice is forming on the tips of my wings
Unheeded warnings, I thought I thought of everything
No navigator to guide my way home
Unladened, empty and turned to stone

A soul in tension thats learning to fly
Condition grounded but determined to try
Cant keep my eyes from the circling skies
Tongue-tied and twisted just an earth-bound misfit, i

Friction lock - set.
Mixture - rich
Propellers - fully forward
Flaps - set - 10 degrees
Engine gauges and suction - check

Mixture set to maximum percent - recheck
Flight instruments...
Altimeters - check both
(garbled word) - on
Navigation lights - on
Strobes - on
(to tower): confirm 3-8-echo ready for departure
(tower): hello again, this is now 129.4
(to tower): 129.4. its to go.
(tower): you may commence your takeoff, winds over 10 knots.
(to tower): 3-8-echo
Easy on the brakes. take it easy. its gonna roll this time.
Just hand the power gradually, and it...

Above the planet on a wing and a prayer,
My grubby halo, a vapour trail in the empty air,
Across the clouds I see my shadow fly
Out of the corner of my watering eye
A dream unthreatened by the morning light
Could blow this soul right through the roof of the night

Theres no sensation to compare with this
Suspended animation, a state of bliss
Cant keep my eyes from the circling skies
Tongue-tied and twisted just an earth-bound misfit, i

```

### Post by money2themax on 2008-12-02
amaroK: Learning To Fly on Pink Floyd on A Momentary Lapse Of Reason

```

Into the distance, a ribbon of black
Stretched to the point of no turning back
A flight of fancy on a wind swept field
Standing alone my senses reeled
A fatal attraction is holding me fast
How can I escape this irresistible grasp?

Can't keep my eyes from the circling sky
Tongue-tied and twisted, just an earth-bound misfit, I

Ice is forming on the tips of my wings
Unheeded warnings, I thought I thought of everything
No navigator to find my way home
Unladened, empty and turned to stone

A soul in tension that's learning to fly
Condition grounded but determined to try
Can't keep my eyes from the circling skies
Tongue-tied and twisted, just an earth-bound misfit, I

Friction lock - set. 
Mixtures - rich
Propellers - fully forward
Flaps - set - 10 degrees
Engine gauges and suction - check
Mixture set to maximum percent - recheck
Flight instruments...
Altimeters - check both
Lights - on
Pilot Heater - on
Strobes - on
Confirm 3-8-Echo ready for departure.
Hello again, this is now 129.4.
129.4. It's to go.
3-8-Echo cleared takeoff, the wind's normal at 10 knots.
3-8-Echo.
Easy on the brakes. Take it easy. It's gonna roll this time.
Just hand the power gradually, and it...

Above the planet on a wing and a prayer,
My grubby halo, a vapor trail in the empty air,
Across the clouds I see my shadow fly
Out of the corner of my watering eye
A dream unthreatened by the morning light
Could blow this soul right through the roof of the night

There's no sensation to compare with this
Suspended animation, a state of bliss
Can't keep my mind from the circling sky
Tongue-tied and twisted, just an earth-bound misfit, I

```

### Post by Piraja on 2008-12-03
> **Gannon8 said:**
> The pot boiling, my dad talking on the phone, my typing, the computer's fan, and my bro. walking across the room.
That's good: sometimes it's worthwhile just to listen to what happens around us turning into an *acousmatic* environment. Sometimes you can just concentrate on the sounds themselves, as if it were *[musique concrète]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Musique_concr%C3%A8te")*.

### Post by Giant Speck on 2008-12-27
I am really, really trying to enjoy Maria Mena's newest album, *Cause and Effect,* but the style is so much different from her earlier albums that it's more annoying than enjoyable.

She has stripped the music down to just her and a piano and some other lighter instrumental sounds.  Her voice is too whispery and shrill.  I hate when she sings like that.

She has proven she can put some real power in her voice.  A couple good examples of that would be "Your Glasses" and "Just Hold Me."

### Post by fballem on 2009-03-31
Pacific Suite by Dan Gibson (Solitudes). When I'm working, I like listening to relaxing music. I have a fair number of the Nature with Music series from Solitudes.

When I'm working, I also listen to Mike Oldfield and an old promotional CD that I received from British Airways called, "A Better World for Business".

When I'm not working, then I listen to Neil Diamond and Sarah Brightman a lot, as well as classical, such as Aaron Copland. I have also recently discovered Il Divo.

Nice thing about a large hard-drive is that I can put all of these on my computer in lossless format - either FLAC or WMA Lossless. The Fluendo codecs play these flawlessly. The medibuntu codecs can't play the WMA Lossless.

Back to work!

### Post by fleaaccela on 2009-06-04
any oldschool hiphop or jazz fans here?

i listen to alot of hiphop from 87-95. a tribe called quest is definitely the greatest of all time. lotta jazz from 58-74. mostly artists who were somehow associated with miles' second quintet or the jazz messengers.

blue note is definately the tops. rarely disappoints. prestige has been doing a great job of reissuing RVG remasters of their catalouge. sony was on top rocking out those infamous CTI records for a while, but i haven't seen anything released in a while.

just got done listening to pete rock and cl smooth's "mecca and the soul brother" in it's entirety earlier today. great album.

The One
Born to bear and bring to all the details of our ending
To write it down for all the world to see

But I forget my pen
S*it the bed again
Typical

### Post by JillSwift on 2009-08-23
For anyone using MPD and as lazy as I am:

```
#!/bin/bash
############################
# MPD song to clipboard
# By JillSwift
# Licence: Public domain
############################

sng=$(mpc --format "[ [%track%. ][%title%]\n [By %artist% ][from '%album%']]|[%file%]\n " | head -2 | sed -e "s/^ //g")

echo -e "$sng"

echo -ne "$sng" | xsel --clipboard

exit 0
```

Run the script, and then just paste into a reply to this thread. :)

### Post by Giant Speck on 2009-08-27
Skillet - Hero

I'm fairly disappointed with Skillet's new album, *Awake*.  The only really good song from the album is Hero and it was released months before the album.  John's voice is far too raspy and strained in this album; it's as if he is trying (really poorly) to channel the musical style of Three Days Grace.

---o0o---
Stuck in the Middle - Gerry Rafferty et al. (ple-ee-ee-ee-eeze)

Ten tunes above^
=========================================================

Bette Davis Eyes - Bonnie Tyler (she's ferocious)
---o0o---
Little Pink Houses - John (Cougar) Mellencamp. (you could stop a clock)
---o0o---
My Lady D'arbarnville - Cat Stevens (why do you grieve me so?)
---o0o---
Rhiannon - Fleetwood Mac - S. Nicks vocals (the sky is starlet/ss)
---o0o---

The Tide is High - Blondie (***)
---o0o---

---o0o---
Slave to Love - Roxy Music - B. Ferry vocals (too young to reason and too grown-up to dream)
---o0o---
So Happy Together - The Kinks (no matter how they toss the dice, it had to be)
---o0o---
You Were Meant for Me - Jewel. (Dreams last so long, even after you're gone)
---o0o---
20 tunes so far on Rhythmbox - just to keep track of how long this is running
=========================================================

Mean to Me - Crowded House (with a black&white tv to stop us from getting lonely)
---o0o---
Blackbird - The Beatles (another favourite - You were only waiting for this moment to be free)

She's not There - Carlos Santana et al. (her eyes were clear and bright)
---o0o---

Take it to the Limit - The Eagles (you know I've always been a dreamer)

### Post by DivineTemplar on 2009-10-01

### Post by toupeiro on 2009-10-29
Master and Slave - Cherry Poppin' Daddies (One of the most lyrically amazing songs I know.)

> No pennies from heaven, no pennies in my hand
Think your drinkin' wine dad it's blood of the lamb
That's no way to treat your son now is it Abraham
After he busted his *** for you
You've never known hunger, never took a risk
Through you know you should, you know you'd never
Raise a fist
If the boss asked you to jump you know
You'd find the nearest cliff
That man talks down to you
And you talk down to me too now

Master and Slave in equal parts
split down the middle drinkin' doubles at the bar
Master and Slave God bless you both
I got two for the price of one
(Such a deal for your only son)

When will this tension be all over
For fallen sons and fathers too
I'm down to my last dime
No faith in mankind
C'mon let's swing into the groove...

Like Jack Horner backed up in a corner
But I never saw a slice of no pie
Too busy standin' in line
Just waitin' for bread
But the father he walks the water
He ain't never tossed a nickel to his son
Ain't I your prodigal boy?
Ain't I your pride and joy?

Friends and Romans I'm your brother
I'm scratchin' to hang on
The pursuit of happiness is
just a carpetbaggers con
When a can of pork and beans
could change my attitude
You won't give it up but I coulda been you
While the masters and slaves scratch
For pieces of the dream
For purple mountain majesties
Whatever the hell that means

They give up on each other
And that's the way they get ahead
But I can still see the stars
Through these red, white, and blue prison bars

Master and Slave in equal parts
split down the middle drinkin' doubles at the bar
Master and Slave God bless you both
I got two for the price of one
(Such a deal for your only son)

### Post by toupeiro on 2009-10-31
While the City Sleeps - MC 900 Foot Jesus

> Stealing down an ally on a cold dark night
I see a halo in the rain around the street light
I stop and look, and listen to the sound
As the raindrops penetrate the silence all around
Alone, I gaze into the glistening street
The distant thunder echoing my heartbeat
Urging me on to a secret goal
Away from the light from this lamp on a pole
So I turn, slip away into the rain
Drifting like a spirit through the shadows in the lane
Clutching the tools of my trade in my hand
An old box of matches and a gasoline can
Darkness envelopes the scene like a shroud
A veil of emptiness hangs from the clouds
Filling up the cracks in this desolate place
Cradled by the night in an icy embrace

Moving to the town like a ghost in the rain
A dim reflection in a dark window pane
Blackness beckons from every side
Creeping all around like an incoming tide
A broken window in an empty house
I slip inside and begin to douse
The whole place with the fuel that will feed the fire
And push back the night, taking me higher
On out of the darkness in a defeaning roar
The match in my hand is the key to the door
A simple turn of the wrist will suffice
To open a passage to paradise
I pause, I think about the past and the gloom
The smell of gasoline permeates the room
Everyone has a little secret he keeps
I light the fires while the city sleeps

(Like the 4th of July)

The match makes a graceful arc to the floor
And time stands still as I turn for the door
Which explodes in a fireball and throws me to the street
I hit the ground running with the flames at my feet
Reaching for the night which recoils from the fire
The raindrops hiss like a devilish choir
Dying in the flames with a terrible sound
Calling all the names of the sleepers all around
But then in the arms of the night, they lay
Their dreams, sprout wings and fly away
Out of the houses in a gathering flock
Swarming overhead as I hurry down the block
I make my escape with the greatest of ease
And savor the darkness, drop to my knees
And the lightless window, my hand on the latch
I reach in my pocket, and pull out a match

(Like the 4th of July) 

---

> **nini9012 said:**

LOL.
I'm listening to "Creeping out Sara" by NOFX!
 ```
I was backstage at a festival in Germany
talking to a cute fair skinned brunette
I asked her if she wanted to have a beer and if she liked our set
I noticed that her hair was longer in the back
I figure that’s cuz she’s Canadian
That’s why I was surprised when she told me she was fully lesbian

That's when I realized it was Sara, or maybe it was Tegan
I'm pretty sure that it was Sara
Cause Johnny Sampson said she was the cooler one

I told her I was a big fan of her band
She asked me if I had a favorite song
I admitted that I'd never actually heard them, but I like k.d. lang

I told her this Jew knows about the Juno’s
And how they got robbed three times in a row
And then I asked her if she knew of anyone who was selling pills or blow

That's when I creeped out Sara or maybe I just pissed her off
When I asked her if her sister and her had ever had a threesome?
Where they both ganged up one girl, a forgy or a fivesome?
Do they think strap-ons are groovy, and had they ever seen the movie
Bound and did they like Jennifer Tilly, did they like Jennifer Tilly?

Sara said she preferred Gina Gershon, Angelina Jolie
In the Gia movie that was on HBO
And “If These Walls Could Talk” made Tegan cry a lot
After that I forgot what she said
That's the problem with Diazepam, so many things I don't remember
But I've got a sinking feeling, it's not that I'm a clairvoyant

But I think that I creeped out Sara, I was creeping out on Sara
I hope it wasn't Tegan, cuz Matt Skiba said she is the cooler one 

```

### Post by ebozzz on 2010-02-04
A **very** eclectic playlist that has music from the following genres:

Reggae (*Lee "Scratch" Perry, Sizzla, Eddy Grant, The Wailers*)
African (*Salif Keita, King "Sunny" Ade*)
Rock (*Peter Frampton, Led Zeppelin, Bad Company, Cream, The Beatles, Paul Rodgers, The Doors*)
Funk / R&B (*Parliament, Prince, The Bar-Kays, George Duke, Stevie Wonder, Average White Band, Con Funk Shun, Angela Bofill, Brick, The Isley Brothers*)
Jazz (*Oscar Peterson Trio, Bud Powell, Thelonious Monk, John Coltrane, Sonny Rollins, Art Blakey, Greg Osby, Miles Davis*)
Jazz-Funk (*Grover Washington, Jr., Hiroshima, Ronnie Laws*)
Hip-Hop (*A Tribe Called Quest*) 
and Pop (*Kraftwerk*). 

You should see the next playlist that is being created..... :D

You're in for surprise
You're in for a shocker
In London town streets
When there's darkness with fog
When you least expect me
And you turn your back

I smile when I'm sneaking
Through shadows by the wall
I laugh when I'm creeping
But you won't hear me at all

All hear my warning
Never turn your back
On the ripper

Never knowing if I'm near

Nocturnal and nameless

Any back alley street
Is where we'll probably meet
Underneath a gas lamp
Where the air's cold and damp
I'm a nasty surprise
I'm a devil in disguise
I'm a footstep at night
I'm a scream of the fright

All hear my warning
Never turn your back

### Post by toupeiro on 2010-04-18
Times, they are a changin' -Bob Dylan

The man wrote this song 46 years ago, and its still so valid..



[I]Come gather 'round people
Wherever you roam
And admit that the waters
Around you have grown
And accept it that soon
You'll be drenched to the bone.
If your time to you
Is worth savin'
Then you better start swimmin'
Or you'll sink like a stone
For the times they are a-changin'.

Come writers and critics
Who prophesize with your pen
And keep your eyes wide
The chance won't come again
And don't speak too soon
For the wheel's still in spin
And there's no tellin' who
That it's namin'.
For the loser now
Will be later to win
For the times they are a-changin'.

Come senators, congressmen
Please heed the call
Don't stand in the doorway
Don't block up the hall
For he that gets hurt
Will be he who has stalled
There's a battle outside ragin'.
It'll soon shake your windows
And rattle your walls
For the times they are a-changin'.

Come mothers and fathers
Throughout the land
And don't criticize
What you can't understand
Your sons and your daughters
Are beyond your command
Your old road is
Rapidly agin'.
Please get out of the new one
If you can't lend your hand
For the times they are a-changin'.

The line it is drawn
The curse it is cast
The slow one now
Will later be fast
As the present now
Will later be past
The order is
Rapidly fadin'.
And the first one now
Will later be last
For the times they are a-changin'.[/I]

### Post by jetsam on 2010-05-16
This one's inevitable.  It is Saturday night: [Hot Patootie]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1vBrSWZnB9c")

Those familiar with the movie might want to avoid the meat coarse.  It's a bit... gamey.

Here's a nice one: [Wouldn't It Be Nice]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L--cqAI3IUI") by The Beach Boys

This is a transplant from the lyrics thread for the hopelessly romantic.  It's almost operatic, almost.  I've heard the song before but didn't know the band and never really listened to the lyrics.  It's something else entirely. [EVANESCENCE-=- My Immortal]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B3YrOlv9Djc")

[Watching the Detectives]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Sed8IjdXdGk") by Elvis Costello, the originator and perfecter of the nerdy sneer.

One nice thing about the internet, it's easy to time shift.  Who's to say which order the reels go, anyways.  [Over at the Frankenstein Place]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GnzQRHoXyxg") acoustic version sung and performed by its creator.

### Post by jetsam on 2010-05-21
[DK California Uber Alles.]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4oJtm5-1cDA")
[Star Holiday Inn Cambodia]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-KTsXHXMkJA")
Devo [Whip It]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IIEVqFB4WUo")

What bands we had!  They hated nostalgia, too.  Trouble for life, basically.

Great Punk.  It gave me the blues.

Blues-- AND TROUBLE!

Keb Mo [Victims of Comfort]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yRHWv2n-NXw").
Corey Harris [Fish Ain't Biting]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3U_Wf1gXKVg")

I love linking to videos online because-- really, there are way too many people in the world think that the blues aren't with us any more.

So anyway.  He paints himself blue and suffers in public.

I suffer privately when I'm able, and have the blues in public because... I'm a bit of an aspiring guitarist and a big ham.  I'm happy.

Don't know about that blue dude.  I'm pretty sure I didn't win, and he very badly lost.

### Post by jetsam on 2010-05-21
Camper Van Beethoven Almost Anything.  

I look at my single solitary great cd and wonder where the rest of the collection got to.

Here's  [Bill.]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aErNjERoLJM")  Warning, not safe for those who are afraid of the word h**l.

Camper Van That Was The Day...  That My Little Dog Lassie--  she sailed...
Off to the moon

The day
the day

[That Was The Day that Lassie Went to The Moon]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s-hmqY2Md4I").

Well it's almost "mean" to leave this one unresolved, but then it's wrong to waste a good thing too.  So you should check out Telephone Free Landslide Victory when you get a chance.

Here's one from YouTube I never heard before:

 Camper Van Beethoven - [One of These Days]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N5-Mxo_fLyE&feature=PlayList&p=01657F7275931487&playnext_from=PL&playnext=1&index=13")

Wow.  Indisputable 4/4... at least at the start.  This band never stands still for long.

### Post by Face-Ache on 2012-05-28
Currently listening to an album by a dude named Michael Kiwanuka. The album is called Home Again, and i guess it's kind of Otis Redding style R&B/Soul, but with awesome modern production.

It's a rich, warm, detailed album. Kiwanuka is actually a session guitarist based in London.

It's one of those albums that you sit down and listen to with a decent nip of good scotch in your hand  :D

Here's a live clip;
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gM0enyfnbD0](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gM0enyfnbD0)

### Post by Mephisto Pheles on 2014-01-17
Steely Dan on Spotify for Linux (Preview). which I didn't know existed until yesterday... 
 installed it on xubuntu 12.04.4 and it runs fine, and seems fully functional for a beta (preview) 
now that their free streaming is no longer restricted/limited I finally gave it a shot.

Anybody else wanna try, it's here
[http://community.spotify.com/t5/Help-Desktop-Linux-Mac-and/Spotify-0-9-4-for-GNU-Linux/td-p/556976](http://community.spotify.com/t5/Help-Desktop-Linux-Mac-and/Spotify-0-9-4-for-GNU-Linux/td-p/556976)

It integrates nicely with the volume applet so you don't have to have the app open all the time.
One ad after 3 tracks is definitely acceptable to me, they're not that intrusive here either, the ads.

### Post by QIII on 2015-09-07
Both of my Grandfathers were from families from Ireland that came to the "Colonies" prior to the Revolution.  They kept the family by Irish by marrying girls coming over from Ireland.  I married a brown-haired cailín from an Irish family from Iowa.

Our kids grew up like I did -- crazy Irish music all the time.

I'm sure my kids think I'm far too old for Flogging Molly and The Young Dubliners.

---

### Post by redbikemaster on 2015-09-07
If you like the music, age is meaningless. I have wide, eccentric musical taste.

---

### Post by QIII on 2015-09-07
The High Kings:  Red Is The Rose

[I]Red is the rose that in yonder garden grows
Fair is the lily of the valley
Clear is the water that flows from the Boyne
But my love is fairer than any.

Come over the hills, my bonnie Irish lass
Come over the hills to your darling
You choose the rose, love, and I'll make the vow
And I'll be your true love forever.[/I]

My favorite song for Mrs QIII.  The night I met her I gave her a red rose and I knew she was the one.

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2015-09-28
I had no idea anyone beyond Australia or New Zealand would have any idea whatsoever who Mi-Sex. Yes, I'm well aware of the details you have posted apart from the non-US stuff. The details you've posted are exaggerated, though. Computer Games was a huge hit here. One or two others maybe. But that was it. They pretty much dribbled into obscurity reasonably quickly. I don't remember another hit of there's and there were certainly bigger bands here, probably ones completely, or little known overseas.

But everyone remembers 'Computer Games' here.

---

### Post by QDR06VV9 on 2015-09-28
Yah! That Era had a bunch of one hit wonders. Still nice to hear but...
Half the bands I listened to sounded really good,  as long as I did not know what they looked like.
For me that Era was a breath of fresh air. until i had seen them then it was a gasp to catch my breath!:D

---

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2015-09-28

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2015-11-07
[Juan Esquivel: Boulevard of Broken Dreams]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=du7EwAnSB8o"). Unfortunately the audio on the link leaves a lot to be desired in comparison to the FLAC I'm listening to.

This cat was on his own musical planet and there are too many gems of his creation to be listed here. Forget genres and what's supposed to be cool and just listen. :)

If you like it, I suggest you head for Esquivel's rendition of 'Lazy Bones' or 'My Blue Heaven' as a further taster ... but as mentioned, there are many examples.

### Post by QDR06VV9 on 2016-01-06
> **yetimon_64 said:**
> Cream, Badge.

Could not help myself here..(Trivia):D
> "Badge" is a song performed by Cream, written by Eric Clapton and George Harrison. It was included as a track on Cream's final album, Goodbye. Peaking at number 60 on Billboard's Hot 100, "Badge" was a minor hit after its release as a single in April 1969. The single was much more successful in the United Kingdom, where it reached #18.

"Badge" was originally an untitled track. During the production transfer for the album Goodbye, the original music sheet was used to produce the liner notes and track listing. The only discernible word on the page was "bridge" (indicating the song's bridge section). **Due to Harrison's handwriting, however, Clapton misread it as "badge" **— and the song was titled soon thereafter.
Grand Kids today..

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2016-02-13
*Heavy Weather, Blackmarket* also Weather Report classics. You surprise me, runrickus. Thought you'd be all over Weather Report like you are over just about every other style of music. :D 

The founding members on keys and wind are genuinely from the old school and trailblazers. History on some of Coltrane's and Miles Davis' classic recordings and performances, etc., eventually evolving into their own group and sound with WR. 

Mind you, they are 'fusion' rather than rock, but there is plenty of rock in fusion!!! At least the WR variety. :D

(PS: If you like WR, check out [the Zawinul Syndicate]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SEsc7YChVmM") (the keyboard player's outfit post-WR, controlled improvisation and not too hard to see who's in control!). Wildness. *Faces and Places* and *My People* are goodies.)

(PPS: Bapoumba, you may be interested to know that I did a comparison and analysis of [WR's 'Teen Town']("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lSUk8bSVHYc") from *Heavy Weather* as one of the components of my honours degree a couple of years ago. 

Transcribed the original bass part then compared with transcriptions and performance techniques of three contemporary versions by other players: [Brian Bromberg]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YG3enZVvxRE"), [Michael Manring]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-w6dtAmBSC4") and [Stephane Manga]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7p7dkY6ikfk"), all totally different takes on the Jaco Pastorius/WR classic (and what would be the point of competing with JPastorius or attempting an exact copy in any case?!?). :)

Probably sounds boring as bat's to many, but I loved it!)

And I've probably gotten carried away, or should be, and blabbered too much information, but I'm a musicologist and I love music. What can I say. Guilty!!! :D

---

(PS: If you like WR, check out [the Zawinul Syndicate]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SEsc7YChVmM") (the keyboard player's outfit post-WR, controlled improvisation and not too hard to see who's in control!). Wildness. *Faces and Places* and *My People* are goodies.)

(PPS: Bapoumba, you may be interested to know that I did a comparison and analysis of [WR's 'Teen Town']("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lSUk8bSVHYc") from *Heavy Weather* as one of the components of my honours degree a couple of years ago. 

Transcribed the original bass part then compared with transcriptions and performance techniques of three contemporary versions by other players: [Brian Bromberg]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YG3enZVvxRE"), [Michael Manring]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-w6dtAmBSC4") and [Stephane Manga]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7p7dkY6ikfk"), all totally different takes on the Jaco Pastorius/WR classic (and what would be the point of competing with JPastorius or attempting an exact copy in any case?!?). :)

Probably sounds boring as bat's to many, but I loved it!)

And I've probably gotten carried away, or should be, and blabbered too much information, but I'm a musicologist and I love music. What can I say. Guilty!!! :D
He He He I know right? I am honestly embarrassed here because I know who all the individual's are just the Name Weather Report escapes me:D
> but I'm a musicologist and I love music. What can I say. Guilty!!! :grin:
Right there with you.:D

### Post by Beautymist on 2016-03-08
silence.

I've been intentionally turning things down lately and listen carefully to the silence. Car passing by in the streets, the hum of the ventilating system in the kitchen. Birds chriping (despite thesnow falling).

It's been an interesting experience, very soothing. 

I can listen to a lot of loud music, too. But I recently went back to regular singing lessons and somehow my ears need to rest. If that makes sense.

---

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2016-03-08
Ear fatigue is a fact. Listening to anything over a long period, not just music, will cause it. Your ears will start to lose 'frequency', in other words, the little hairs that pick up frequencies in your ears will start to 'lie down' and you will lose certain frequencies. Give them a break and voila, you can hear the oboe again! Or the double bass drum ...

Just like taking a break from sitting down once an hour, same with music. Good idea to give them a break around an hour if you are doing active listening (passive not so bad, radio on in the distance, for instance).

PS: Very nontechnical explanation of ear fatigue but based on facts. :)

### Post by QDR06VV9 on 2016-04-13
801 - "Tomorrow Never Knows" (1976) 

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-Usb26HywtU](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-Usb26HywtU)

A refreshing remake...I liked it.
Experimental rock band formed in 1976 after the temporary disbanding of Roxy Music by Phil Manzanera, Brian Eno, Bill MacCormick, Francis Monkman, Simon Phillips and Lloyd Watson. The group performed three critically acclaimed concerts at Norfolk, the Reading festival and London's Queen Elizabeth Hall. The final concert was recorded and released as 801 Live. A studio album; Listen Now - recorded at various dates

### Post by QDR06VV9 on 2016-05-02
> **haplorrhine said:**
> The Velvet Underground - Sunday Morning

speakers packed away

Wow I have not heard this since the 70's..good choice.
In late 1966, "Sunday Morning" was the final song to be recorded for The Velvet Underground & Nico. It was requested by Tom Wilson, who thought the album needed another song with lead vocals by Nico with the potential to be a successful single. The final master tape of side one of the album shows "Sunday Morning" only penciled in before "I'm Waiting for the Man". But when it came time to record it, Lou Reed sang the lead vocal. Nico would instead sing backing vocals on the song.
> Sunday mornin', praise the dawnin'
It's just a restless feelin', by my side
Early dawnin', Sunday mornin'
It's just the wasted years so close.

TV adverts, super markets, medical centres..........and is it all 'music'? Music or sound, why is it there?Yes, silence is hard to find. ### Post by Bucky Ball on 2016-06-07
> **grandad67 said:**
> ...and is it all 'music'?

The answer is completely subjective. As the thread is about what are you listening to now, what are you listening to now, actively or passively? If I listen carefully I can hear lots of things, and I have no TV or radio or music playing. :)

There is no such thing as silence if you listen hard enough. (Unless, of course, you are in a specifically designed environment which is intended to limit sound volume, but even then, there is still sound. I can't imagine, even for a deaf person, there would be no sound. Probably internal. I'll ask next time I meet a deaf person.)

### Post by Bucky Ball on 2016-10-05
You love Icehouse? In case you were unaware, they started as [The Flowers]("https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Icehouse_(band)"). Check [this out]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Gxjqn7gz2xI").

Have a look at some of their other stuff under the Flowers name. The full 'Icehouse' *album*, by Flowers, is on Youtube. :)

I liked them under both names but more then. Still, being in Australia at the time these guys arrived on the scene guess it was different than otherwise. I was an impressionable young lad when these guys arrived.

### Post by C.S.Cameron on 2019-11-11
**The Metal Worker**

Our next to next door neighbor is a metal worker.
He starts off with discarded oil drums and beats them into begging bowls for the Monks.
This is a noisy profession. there are not many people left doing this.
He used to start about 6:00 AM and beat until the mid afternoon.
Lately he does not start until 8:00 or 9:00 and finished around noon.
It is like living by train tracks, after a few years you hardly notice the noise.

Nowadays, (unlike Cambodia), people bring their offering, (dana), to the temple.
The Monks still must beg and they eat from their bowls, they do not take anything not personally offered to them.
They must finish their meal before noon.

---

### Post by jetsam on 2019-11-11
There's a whole genre of music built around repurposed oil drums...  centered in the Caribbean, but with influences from everywhere as is usually the case:
[The Steelpan: A Gem from Trinidad and Tobago! | Josanne Francis | TEDxBethesda]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fwmgYrWtN9M")

---

### Post by C.S.Cameron on 2019-11-11
**Poya Day**

Today is Full Moon or Poya Day.
It is a holiday in Buddhist countries.

Our house is close to a Buddhist temple.
At 5:00 AM the drumming started.
After about fifteen minutes the drumming changed to chanting.
The chanting is in Pali an ancient language related to Sanskrit.
The subject of the chants is the Tipi&#7789;aka or Pali Canon.
The sound is beautiful.
Later it will change back to drums with occasional lecturing.
The lectures are in Sinhala, a modern language derived from Pali.   
This will go on all day long.
A couple weeks ago it was Kathina, the end of rainy season.
The drums and chanting lasted about four days.

---

### Post by C.S.Cameron on 2019-11-14
**The Wedding**

Guests were greeted by traditional dance in the hotel lobby, with drums and cymbal sounds.
The couple moved to an area by the ocean.
More traditional dancing, somersaults, spins and twirls, to the sound of drums and a background of the surf.
The couple sat in front of a civil officer in silence for about a half hour.
The sun was raging.
At precisely 9:38 AM the papers were signed.
The couple moved to a small stage.
There was chanting in Sinhala with occasional drumming.
At exactly 10:12 AM rings were exchanged. 
More chanting, the meaning I understood to be "You are not gaining a son, you are loosing a daughter".
Everyone moved inside for traditional breakfast of milk rice, chili sambal, and sweets.
Then up to the penthouse for lunch and contemporary Sri Lankan music, a mix of traditional music, rock and metal.
The men then migrated to the veranda, for whisky ice and soda.
The bride and her maids did a choreographed dance, very nice.
The men took over the dance floor and the contemporary music lasted the rest of the day.

In a large clear plastic cup,

My dog barking viciously at cable/phone repairmen who thought is was alright to just enter my back yard without knocking on the door. ### Post by jetsam on 2019-11-21

The B-52s - Rock Lobster[/URL]

[Mesopotamia]("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0FyLcHxbSRk")

**Edit for egghead analysis:** How did they score such mainstream success when their cultural roots arose from gay sub-culture?  Answer: it was the 70's, the sexuality was ignorable, you could pretend they were hetero-normative if you wanted to.  Straight people probably assumed they were straight, gay people probably assumed they were gay... Compared to glam-rock from the early 70's, everything new wave seemed straight at the time.

### Post by QIII on 2022-05-27
Until a few moments ago, me practicing the final guitar arrangement of the lullaby I wrote for my grandson.  It moves through four fairly short themes and uses virtually the entire range of an acoustic guitar short 7 frets on the high E string.

Had to play through with the nail on my right index finger worn a bit beyond where I like them to be.  At my age (and owning a farm!) the nails get brittle and wear or break, so I have to reinforce them with a half-moon sliver of fake acrylic nail on the tip.  Acrylic wears pretty fast compared to my nails when I was young.  Sigh.  Don't laugh.  James Taylor sometimes uses *fiberglass!*
